Compound 6a (10H-benzo[i]quino[2,3,4-kl]acridin-10-one), m.p. 255°C verified in paper 102; evidence quote present verbatim. Self-flag was a strict compound-token lint false positive — context unambiguously identifies 6a.

Value [Ch][Ac]/[Ch][Iv] Tm,eu 39°C is real in Table 3 of liu_2020, but the evidence_quote stitches the table header to that row, skipping intervening [Ch][Ac]/[Ch][Ib] and [Ch][Ac]/[Ch][2mb] rows; not a contiguous substring.

The cited section in paper 079 characterizes compound 10, 1-(2,4-Difluorophenyl)-2-(1H-1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)ethanol, with mp 119–121 °C; the row's generic name 'fluorinated nitroazole derivative' is not specific enough and is incorrect for this compound.

Value [Ch][Ib]/[Ch][Iv] Tm,eu 48°C is real in Table 3 of liu_2020, but the evidence_quote stitches header to that row, skipping intermediate table rows; not a contiguous substring (agent's own self-flag is correct).

Compound 26 mp 62–66°C is correct in 2019_Marek paper, but the evidence_quote as a long natural-prose string is not a contiguous substring of the PDF (two-column layout splits the text); pdftotext extraction interleaves columns.
Compound 4ag/4da, mp 154.9–200.0 °C verified verbatim in paper 171; midpoint 177.45 °C correctly computed; DOI molecules29122917 present.

Compound 2 (6-Nitro-2H-chromen-2-one), mp: 199–201 °C verified verbatim in paper 174; DOI ijms25126803 present.

Compound 6 (2-Methoxy-4-O-methoxymethylbenzaldehyde), Mp: 73-74°C verified verbatim in Plourde 2002 paper (Molecules 2002, 7, 315-319). DOI suffix 70200315 does not literally appear in the PDF text, but paper identity is unambiguous via title/authors/journal/year/pages.
AASD phase change temperature 93.3 °C verified in liu_2023 (DOI d3ra01118f present); evidence quote is contiguous in PDF text.
Compound 10a (6-Amino-2H-chromen-2-one), mp: 171–173 °C verified verbatim in paper 174.

Compound 4c in paper 170 is actually 'Triisopropylsilyl (E)-6-(4-Hydroxy-6-methoxy-7-methyl-3-oxo-1,3-dihydroisobenzofuran-5-yl)-4-methylhex-4-enoate'; the row uses generic 'Silyl ester MPA analogue 4c' which is not a specific compound name and the evidence_quote does not contain the row's compound_name token (agent self-flag confirmed).
Compound 6f, M.P.: 130–132 °C verified verbatim in paper 068; DOI molecules31050844 present.
Compound 13a, mp:128–130 °C verified verbatim in paper 174.

Compound 22 mp 179–183°C is real in 2019_Marek paper, but the evidence_quote as a long natural-prose reconstruction is not a contiguous substring of pdftotext extraction due to two-column layout (text interleaves across columns).

SD/E TM 23.5 °C is real in Table 1 of zhang_2021, but the evidence_quote is a flattened/constructed representation of the multi-row table header + SD/E row joined together; not a contiguous substring.
Compound 3d, mp: 212-14°C (shorthand for 212–214) verbatim in paper 138.
DOI present in paper, evidence quote is a contiguous substring of article_text.txt, value 68-70 °C appears in quote, compound 6b confirmed by surrounding context.

Paper file is HTML; the CSV's evidence_quote (rendered-text form) is not a contiguous substring of the raw HTML source — heading + characterization paragraph are separated by intervening
//
markup. Underlying data (compound M20, mp 195-198) is present in the paper.
DOI present, quote is contiguous in article_text.txt, value 187°C (decomp.) and compound 3c in quote.
DOI present, quote contiguous in article_text.txt, value 234-236 °C and compound 11c in quote.

Quote is the Table 1 row containing 'Methyl 2-benzamido-2-(phenylamino)acetate ( 2 ) 124-126 48 80 6.22'; the °C unit is in the column header 'm.p. (°C)' shown in the table caption; numeric value 124-126 is in the quote and matches value_raw 124-126 °C.
DOI present, contiguous quote in article_text.txt, value 98.11 °C is in quote, compound 'purified lactide' is the subject.

PMC3692303 substring is present in the paper text. The quote is the Table 1 paragraph; the 60 mol% NbCl5 row reads '60 51' and 'Melting point (°C)' is the column header in the quote — numeric mp 51 is supported.

The quote and immediately preceding synthesis description (Chloro(dimethylsulfide)gold(I) added; final NHC-gold(I) product) are for an NHC-gold(I) tetrafluoroborate complex, NOT for a platinum(II) NHC complex as the compound_name claims. Self-flag confirmed.

Paper source is the HTML file; the raw HTML has 'Mp: 105-106<sup>o</sup>C' so the CSV quote 'Mp: 105-106 o C' is not a contiguous substring of the HTML source even with whitespace/hyphen normalization. Underlying data is correct.

HTML source has the data with interspersed , , tags ('compound 2 at 90% yield; mp: 210-211 °C'); CSV evidence_quote in rendered-text form is not a contiguous raw-HTML substring. Underlying compound 2 / mp 210-211 is correct.
DOI present, contiguous quote in article_text.txt, value 99 °C and compound 11 in quote.

DOI present, quote contiguous in article_text.txt, value 220-221 °C in quote, compound AL8 / N'-(1-(4-nitrophenyl)ethylidene)... in quote.
DOI present, contiguous quote, value >300 °C and compound 6a in quote.
DOI present, contiguous quote, value 209-213 °C and compound 8a in quote.

DOI present, contiguous quote in article_text.txt, value 180-182 °C and compound 4h in quote.

DOI present; quote 'product in DMSO. (Yield: 89%; m.p: 287 - 288°C). 1 H NMR ...' is a contiguous substring (after hyphen-folding) of article_text.txt. Compound Ia is identified by the surrounding paragraph (Ia synthesis). Numeric mp 287-288 is in quote.
DOI present, contiguous quote in article_text.txt, value 180.33 °C in quote, compound 3b in quote.

source_url is 'legacy:manifest_184', not a DOI/PMC/PMID — it cannot be substring-verified against the paper file. The paper PDF (Moncho 2021) does contain the compound and mp >265 °C dec., but the quote span is interrupted by two-column PDF text flow so it is not a contiguous substring of the pdftotext output. Self-flag confirmed.

Quote is an exact raw-HTML snippet (intentionally), so it IS a contiguous substring of the HTML source. Compound '3-(3'-Nitrophenyl)-6-(thiophen-2''-yl)pyridazine 3e' and mp 245-248 °C are in the snippet.

DOI present, contiguous quote in article_text.txt covering the descriptive paragraph + Table 1; row II 'C17H16N4O4 ... 112-114 Yellowish white' with M.P (C°) as column header; compound 'bis(3-nitrobenzylidene)-1,3-diaminopropane' is named in quote.

DOI present, contiguous quote, value 186 °C and compound Chol-phthalonitrile in quote.

Compound 2a (1-pentafluorophenyl-1H-pyrrole-2-carbaldehyde) mp 77-79 °C verified verbatim in PMC6146868; DOI 10.3390/80700536 present in paper text.

The aldimine in the paper is prepared from cyclohexylamine + butyraldehyde (giving N-butylidenecyclohexylamine), not the row's N-benzylidene-2-ethylcyclohexylamine; bp 80-84 °C/20 mm Hg is for the wrong compound.
Compound 5j (3,5-Diamino-4-[(3,4-Dichlorophenyl)Diazinyl]-N-Tosyl-1H-Pyrazole-1-Carboxamide) mp 291-292 °C verified verbatim in PMC12943507 (DOI ph19020239 present).

The evidence quote concatenates Table 2 header tokens with the β=15 data row across multiple rows of the PDF table; the verbatim sequence is not a contiguous substring of the extracted text (Tp 297.99 itself is correct in Table 2).
Compound M9 (Bromophenyl/morpholinosulfonyl thiopyrimidine acetamide) mp 218-222 °C verified verbatim in khalifa_2024 HTML; DOI molecules29194778 present.

Paper labels compound 3a as the systematic name '(E)-6-(4-((tert-Butyldimethylsilyl)oxy)-6-methoxy-7-methyl-3-oxo-1,3-dihydroisobenzofuran-5-yl)-4-methylhex-4-enoic acid'; the row's 'Desilylated MPA analogue 3a' is a description that does not match the IUPAC name in the paper, though mp 122-124 °C and the serial code 3a are confirmed.
